title: using reference package to parse
body:
Follow-up to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/6872 (Rebranched)

Uses reference package to parse as requested by @vrothberg 

Signed-off-by: Parker Van Roy <pvanroy@redhat.com>

comments.body: Rebasing the PR should turn the tests green :+1:

I guess the rebase went wrong (34 commits added). I usually rebase with
```
# update the remotes
$ git update update
# rebase
$ git pull --rebase upstream master
```

Alternatively:
```
git fetch upstream
git rebase upstream/master
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *ParkerVR* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The CI errors look legitimate. You can run a specific file of the e2e tests (e.g., the failing `rmi_test.go`) like that:
```
$ GOPATH=~/go go test -v test/e2e/libpod_suite_test.go test/e2e/common_test.go test/e2e/config.go test/e2e/config_amd64.go test/e2e/rmi_test.go
```

A very quick look suggests that we need to handle `<none>` but there might be more.
